title: Floater squid

The '''floater squid''' was one of two [[Diet/Legends|predatory]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Floater (creature)|floater]] creatures that lived in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]]. Three hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in size, its resilient body consisted of an elongated gasbag with [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] attached to it, and it could reach a speed of three hundred [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]]. It was an opportunistic predator that attacked anything regardless of size, which included the other non-[[Plant/Legends|herbivorous]] floater, the [[floater shark]], as well as [[Starship/Legends|starships]], including the craft of [[Corusca gem/Legends|Corusca gem]] [[Corusca fishermen|fishermen]].

==Biology and appearance==
The floater squid was one of<ref name="GG2-2" /> twelve known [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="CSWE floater">{{CSWECite|I|281|floater (1)}}</ref> of [[Floater (creature)|floater]], a type of creature that resided in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin Prime]]<ref name="GG2-2" /> of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Its body consisted of a huge,<ref name="GG2-2" /> elongated<ref name="GG2">''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]''</ref> gasbag with ten to fifty<ref name="GG2-2" /> large<ref name="GG2" /> [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] suspended from it. The [[Squid/Legends|squid]] reached three hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in size, and it was vulnerable to [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire and ramming by vehicles, with smaller [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] not affecting it. A hit on the gasbag would cause the floater squid to lose its altitude. The squid moved through Yavin Prime's atmosphere by drawing in air and then ejecting it, allowing it to reach a speed of three hundred [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]].<ref name="GG2-2" />

==Behavior==
A voracious [[Diet/Legends|predator]], the floater squid was one of two species of floater that preyed on<ref name="GG2-2" /> the ten other species, members of which ranged in length from thirty to fifteen hundred meters.<ref name="CSWE floater" /> Constantly on the [[Hunting/Legends|prowl]], the squid was willing to attack anything of any size, including the thirty-to-fifty-meter–long predatory [[floater shark]] and [[Starship/Legends|starships]] traveling through Yavin Prime's atmosphere. The latter included the [[Trawler/Legends|trawlers]] and [[Skiff/Legends|skiffs]] used by the [[Corusca fishermen|fishermen]] harvesting the planet's valuable [[Corusca gem/Legends|Corusca gems]]. In particular, the floater squids were apparently attracted by the energy pulses of the [[energy purse seine]]s used by the fishermen.<ref name="GG2-2" />

==History==
{{RPGseed}}
At one point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Dates">[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin'', Second Edition]] is set between the events of {{Film|IV}} and {{Film|V}}, which corresponds to between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Standard century/Legends|centennial]] [[Reproduction/Legends|breeding]] season of Yavin Prime's [[Plant/Legends|herbivorous]] floaters commenced, causing in them heightened aggression. The breeding season and the activity of floater squids both caused considerable difficulties for a team of Corusca [[Gemstone/Legends|gem]] fishermen attempting to poach on the gem-fishing territory controlled by the [[Damarind Corporation]].<ref name="GG2-2" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The floater squid—though not given a formal name at the time—was introduced in ''[[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG2" /> The creature was subsequently identified by name in [[Jonatha Ariadne Caspian]]'s [[1995]] [[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin (Second Edition)|Second Edition]] to the book. The Second Edition also included a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure idea that involved the appearance of floater squids. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="GG2-2" />
